programmes.show_description: The centuries-old secrets behind making frankfurters are revealed. Plus, sniper rifles are made to hit a target from a thousand yards. S8, Ep17
The world's biggest fish market feeds Japan's insatiable appetite for seafood. Plus, the trunk of a giant tree is transformed into cricket bats. S8, Ep18
Tony's Dream Land Rover 2A. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley trade up to an iconic off roader, a Series 2A Land Rover, for ex-soldier Tony. S1, Ep1
Chris's Dream Ford Escort Mk2. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley are on a mission to trade up to a classic Mk2 Ford Escort for office manager, Chris. S1, Ep2
Somerset. Henry and Sam scour Britain's sheds for vehicles to buy, restore and flog for a tidy profit. In Somerset they find Norton motorbikes, Italian mopeds, and French go-karts. S1, Ep13
Hertfordshire. The guys are in Hertfordshire, finding vintage cars, tractors and rare British bikes from the 1950s. When the haggling starts, things don't turn out quite as expected. S1, Ep14
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including boat radar mounts made? S26, Ep8
A look at how everyday items including Asian noodles and rotary engines are made, revealing the unique production processes used in making them. S26, Ep9
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Bicycle expert Tim is tasked with repairing a rusty penny-farthing. Leather worker Suzie restores a briefcase, while Steve renovates a clocking-in clock. S4, Ep9
Drew's son Tom is keen to learn more about the trade. They visit Shepton Mallet Flea market in Somerset, where Tom spots a stunning painting. S17, Ep6
Drew's ability to work under pressure is tested by talented dealer, Alasdair. In a disused church, Drew bids on an elaborate Victorian mirror. S17, Ep7
The Dirt Dogs are determined to get the gold-tally up after last year's devastating season. Meanwhile, the Scrappers are stuck building their machine leaving them behind schedule. S3, Ep1
The Dirt Dogs are under more pressure when their plant doesn't deliver the gold they're expecting. Meanwhile, the Scrappers are in trouble before they've even started mining. S3, Ep2
The Bushmen stay up late to drive out poachers from their mine. Plus, Col Duff reunites with Greg in one last push to find opal before the summer heat gets too much. S3, Ep19
The Young Guns risk it all to find rare pineapple opal. Meanwhile, the Boulder Boys take on deadly ground and the Bushmen face a race to the hospital. S3, Ep20
Bicycle expert Tim is tasked with repairing a rusty penny-farthing. Leather worker Suzie restores a briefcase, while Steve renovates a clocking-in clock. S4, Ep9
Cotswolds. Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds, where they discover sheds full of stationary engines, obscure agricultural implements and some surprising collections of memorabilia. S1, Ep15
West Sussex. Henry and Sam uncover vintage treasures in West Sussex. They go behind the scenes at one of Britain's most famous bike shops and find two extraordinary hidden gems. S1, Ep16
Dave / Cumbria. Henry and Fuzz head for Cumbria where Dave's mega-shed awaits. Henry is lost for words but once the haggle begins, what will he decide on? S5, Ep19
George / Dorset. Henry and Fuzz explore George's automobilia collection in Dorset. Henry's in the mood for a SPQR: Small Profit Quick Return. Can he strike a good deal? S5, Ep20
He Shoots, He Scores. Angus visits an ex-soldier with a collection of vintage Action Man figures. A football fanatic wins big when he sells his Crystal Palace memorabilia. S5, Ep19
Battle Stations. Angus is called in to auction a massive collection of 20,000 model soldiers. And a woman needs to sell her colossal doll's house before retiring. S5, Ep20
A Rebel Without A Cause? Natasha and Ishy take on the penultimate leg of their trip. Natasha buys a couple of antique jugs, and Ishy purchases a beautiful Art Nouveau charger. S27, Ep19
The Witching Hour. Natasha Raskin Sharp and Ishy Khan kick their final leg off in Derbyshire. With a spectacular auction finale in Bristol, who will come out on top? S27, Ep20
The Beaver & A Russian Egg. Angus uncovers expensive oak and a Fabergé egg in Herefordshire, while rare single malt whiskey means big bids and spirited surprises! S6, Ep19
Patricia visits silversmith Brendon in the hopes he can restore an old family brush and comb set. Meanwhile, Dominic's skills are put to the ultimate test. S5, Ep35
A music expert attempts to save a vintage guitar that hasn't been played in 40 years. Meanwhile, Steve brings a well-travelled time piece back to life. S5, Ep36
Powerboats are built to ride waves faster than cars on the road. The secrets behind Swiss watches are revealed. Workers create high-quality wallpaper. S8, Ep19
The world's leading submarine rescue specialists from several countries participate in a training exercise. Workers manufacture all-wheel drive cars. S8, Ep20
How is rubber made into chewing gum? How are fish hooks strong enough to catch a shark? How are the models built at the world's largest Legoland? S9, Ep1
How do they build Formula 1 racing cars? How is the Leatherman, America's best-selling multi-tool made? How do they create classic Finnish Aalto glassware? S9, Ep2
Harlen's Dream Nissan Skyline. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley trade up to iconic Japanese sports car, the Nissan Skyline, for photographer Harlen. S1, Ep3
Adam's Dream TVR Cerbera. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley trade up to a British muscle car, the TVR Cerbera, for electrical engineer Adam. S1, Ep4
Cotswolds. Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds, where they discover sheds full of stationary engines, obscure agricultural implements and some surprising collections of memorabilia. S1, Ep15
West Sussex. Henry and Sam uncover vintage treasures in West Sussex. They go behind the scenes at one of Britain's most famous bike shops and find two extraordinary hidden gems. S1, Ep16
A look at how everyday items including steel pulleys and acetate eyewear are made, revealing the materials and unique production processes used in making them. S26, Ep10
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including gyroscopic stabilizers made? S26, Ep11
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Chase, Buy, Repeat! A client of Wayne's wants one of every production model Ford from 1935-40. First stop is the Ft. Lauderdale sale where bidding is as hot as the Florida sun. S6, Ep9
Green Is the New Black. Wayne's client has a 1973 Daytona Spyder painted 'restoration red'. Not a look that will stand out at the Cavallino Classic, a prestigious Ferrari concourse. S6, Ep10
Wet, Wild & Willys! Wayne auctions off a number of offbeat rides, including a Willys MB Jeep, a Jones Grand Prix Hydroplane boat and a nostalgic Ford F250 Good Humor Truck. S6, Ep11
Overheating in Oregon. Wayne flies out to Oregon for a killer find, an untouched 1932 Stutz DV32. But can he get it back up and running, before selling it on for a profit? S6, Ep12
1985 Maserati Biturbo. Mike discovers a 1985 Maserati Bi-Turbo that was converted to electric in the early 90s. Can they work on their first ever electric project and still profit? S13, Ep16
1987 Alfa Romeo Spider Quadrifoglio. Mike and Ant take on a 1987 Alfa Romeo Spider Quadrifoglio with a rare hardtop. It needs extensive repairs, including a rebuild of the propshaft. S14, Ep11
Fiat Dino. Mike is in northern Italy to find a Fiat Dino 2400 Coupe. But Dino parts prices are high - will Edd be able to fix it up without blowing the budget? S9, Ep1
Ferrari 308 GT4 - Part 1. Mike and Edd really blow the budget on this one, as they get their hands on an amazing Ferrari 308 GT4. But have they taken on too much? S6, Ep11
Ferrari 308 GT4 - Part 2. The Ferrari 308 GT4 is shaping up nicely - and Mike is still more excited than a giddy schoolboy that they own a Ferrari! But can he sell it for a profit? S6, Ep12
Fiat 124. Mike finds a 1972 Fiat 124 Spider that's all original but the driving experience needs a refresh. He gets crafty on the interior while Ant fixes a leaky differential. S15, Ep22
1991 Alfa Romeo 164l. Mike tracks down a rare '91 Alfa Romeo 164L. He loves the look, but Ant has his work cut out for him. The car has perfect bodywork, but it has a leaking engine. S15, Ep5
Fiat Coupe 20 Valve Turbo. Mike and Elvis take on the 'baby Ferrari' but with a tiny budget. Can the guys make this old school classic car a rival to its Italian cousin? S17, Ep3
Richard Gaunt. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's Staffordshire shed, uncovering an Excelsior and Mobylette as they dive into a treasure trove of two-wheeled gems. S7, Ep12
Graham Streeter. Henry and Fuzz visit Graham's Essex shed, packed with bikes, and uncover a Suzuki GSX-R750 Slabside, Henry's dream machine and a true Japanese icon. S7, Ep13
Jeff Maggs. Henry and Fuzz visit Jeff's tractor-packed shed in Chepstow and uncover a Grey Fergie, the iconic Ferguson TE20 that helped transform farming forever. S7, Ep14
Rick Kenway. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's eclectic shed in Hook, packed with bikes, cars and curiosities, and choose a classic Harley-Davidson Sportster to save. S7, Ep15
Dave McNicholas. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave's Derby shed and pick a classic Triumph Daytona, a British icon with racing heritage, for their next restoration challenge. S7, Ep16
John Sheppard. Henry and Fuzz head to Towcester to buy John's Allis-Chalmers crawler. They are determined to revive this classic machine. S7, Ep17
Ollie Turney. Henry and Fuzz visit Ollie to inspect a tired 1955 BSA bike. Though faded and neglected, this legendary machine still boasts heaps of charm. S7, Ep18
Vek McGuire. Henry and Fuzz head to London to meet Vek and uncover a tired 1937 James motorcycle. Drawn in by its graceful lines, the guys get to work. S7, Ep19
H Is For Help! With the workshop haemorrhaging money, Richard brings in help. A merchandising scheme backfires when Lewis massively over-orders stock. S5, Ep4
Wood restorer Will attempts to bring an old fishing box back to its former glory. Meanwhile, Julie and Amanda work together to revive a badly damaged doll. S5, Ep47
The expert team restore treasured items including a 1940s handcrafted leather football, a beautifully decorated broken bowl and a miniature spice box. S8, Ep9
The Beaver & A Russian Egg. Angus uncovers expensive oak and a Fabergé egg in Herefordshire, while rare single malt whiskey means big bids and spirited surprises! S6, Ep19
No Risk No Reward. The Opal Lovers excavate old drives in search of red crystal opal. Plus, mechanical problems are once again giving the Mooka Boys a headache. S8, Ep17
